replies.msg:
如果你不想看文
直接看這條片就可以了
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hdnUCfSTZ1c
也可以看參考書
例如
John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher - Debating Same-Sex Marriage (2012)

觀點一：「同性戀違反自然定律」
反駁一：
a. 自然主義的謬誤
https://www.britannica.com/topic/naturalistic-fallacy
自然天性與道德規範無關
正如David Hume所講
單從「實然」命題不能推導出「應然」命題

b. 退一步而言，同性性行為於大自然亦很常見
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Homosexual_behavior_in_animals

對於「自然與否」的詳細論述，見：
John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher - Debating Same-Sex Marriage (2012) (p.33)

觀點二：「同性戀係病」
反駁二：一早唔係病 閣下思想仍停留在1973年前
https://www.psychiatry.org/news-room/apa-blogs/apa-blog/2016/03/homosexuality-as-a-mental-disorder-simply-not-backed-up-by-science

https://www.nytimes.com/1973/12/16/archives/psychiatrists-in-a-shift-declare-homosexuality-no-mental-illness.html


觀點三：「婚姻定義就是一男一女結合」
反駁三：以前唔係、現在唔係、未來亦唔應該係

以前
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hdnUCfSTZ1c

現在
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Status_of_same-sex_marriage

何況為何定義不可以改？以前大清律例夠話男人可以一夫多妻，現在無人會咁講

詳細論述見:
John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher - Debating Same-Sex Marriage (2012) (p.21-44)
https://global.oup.com/academic/product/debating-same-sex-marriage-9780199756315?cc=hk&lang=en&;

觀點四：「點解同性戀者要咁揚，摺埋咪得囉」
反駁四：
咁異性戀情侶使唔使摺埋？
婚姻自由同戀愛自由都是基本自由
點解要因為性向而有所隱藏

觀點五：「同性戀者令大多數人覺得好嘔心，所以佢地要摺埋」
反駁四：
個人喜惡不足以作為干預他人自由的理由
正如你不能因為極不同意一個人的言論而禁止他說話，因為他有言論自由（除非有極逼切且合理的理由，例如他的言論會對其他人造成即時傷害，e.g.煽動暴力）
可參考J.S. Mill- On Liberty

觀點六：「同性戀者會令人類無法繁衍後代，繼而令人類滅亡」

反駁六：
不會，因為異性戀者可以繼續結婚生子
而且同性戀者可以選擇人工受孕

觀點七：「你依家即係逼我接受同性戀者啊？」

反駁七：
No
你可以選擇繼續不接受（not accept）不認同（not approve）同性戀
但係你必須承認(recognise)並尊重(respect)同性戀者與其他人一樣
都係社群裡面的公民，享有與其他人一樣 平等的基本自由

觀點八：「接受同性戀的理據，邏輯上就等同接受人獸交」
反駁八：

// I think it’s pretty obvious that bestiality doesn’t qualify.
Actually, I thought it was pretty obvious until I had a student push the point in one of my classes. “What about a really intelligent chimpanzee?” he asked with apparent seriousness. But Jonathan Rauch, who was visiting the class via audio conference at the time, settled the question nicely: “And when the spouse is in the hospital on life support,” Rauch responded, “and the doctor calls and asks what to do, what does the really intelligent chimpanzee say?”//

(John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher, p.65-66)
push
push

質疑：支持同性戀logically entails 亂倫合法化

反駁：
// The reason why straight people should not marry relatives is not just because
their of spring might have birth defects (a problem which can be anticipated via genetic testing, and which doesn’t apply past childbearing age), but also because of the ways in which sex changes the dynamics of family life...
...There is another important disanalogy between banning incestuous marriage and banning same-sex marriage. The incest ban means that every person is forbidden to marry some people—a relatively small group—with whom he might fall in love: his relatives. By contrast, the same-sex marriage ban means that gay people are forbidden to marry anyone with whom they might fall in love. Unlike the incest ban, it reduces their pool of available marriage partners to zero—an infinitely greater restriction.// (p.66-67)

John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher - Debating Same-Sex Marriage (2012)
不了

單純問下
如果我係唔鍾意同性戀既
但我覺得佢應該有佢地同一般人一樣既權利
而我自己行為上就會孤立同排斥佢
咁樣算唔算係歧視？
我知道呢樣野係存在 亦唔會話佢地唔啱
只係觀感上接受唔到
想係自己生命中排除呢一樣野
有咩睇法？
亂倫合法化都可以用呢d論點

如果你唔同人溝通嘅出發點係因為同性戀
就已經構成歧視
如果係因為個位同性戀者個人性格問題而唔想有任何接觸或者溝通就唔係歧視
因為你心底對同性戀有份抵觸
唔會願意接觸佢地
當然同性戀者都唔會強逼你要同佢地溝通
始終佢地都會察覺到邊啲人係接受自己
但係依照你個人既心理 如果因為對方係同性戀而唔想溝通已經係歧視嘅一種 當然唔係話嚴重個隻 即係言語或者行動侮辱等

replies.msg:
That is not marriage. By definition, marriage is a civil union which recognize the rights and obligations of persons. You cannot find rights and obligations in two beasts, because they have none.
Another problem is, how can you determined what is civilized and what is not? What is "civilized"? We evolve and live. They evolve and live. They and we live simultaneously on the planet. If a group can live, that means the Nature select it. That's what I know.
If you cannot explain why the definition of civilization follows your criterion, I must not accept it. Also, I do not see polygamy is an old fashion. Islamic people are polygamist, and from ancient China it has already been monogamy.

replies.msg:
歧視呢個議題確實麻煩
因為制度上可以有歧視（直接歧視與間接歧視）

／／現行的反歧視條例對直接歧視和間接歧視的定義，作出了清晰的區別。直接歧視
是指當某人基於某種特定特質（受保障特徵）12如性別、懷孕、婚姻狀況、殘疾、
家庭崗位和種族而受到較差待遇。間接歧視則指當一項條件或要求（ 如規則、
政策、措施、準則或程序）一律應用到所有人身上，但擁有上述「受保障特徵」
而又能夠符合該條件或要求的人士，比例遠遜於沒有該「受保障特徵」的人士。
此外，有關投訴必須證明如此安排對擁有上述「受保障特徵」人士不利，以及有
關要求或條件欠缺理據（如有合理目的和相稱比例)。／／
http://www.eoc.org.hk/eoc/upload/ResearchReport/20161251750347478561.pdf

而制度以外日常生活中亦可以有歧視
你一個人排斥同性戀者當然沒什麼影響（儘管未必合理）
但係當整個社會大多數人都排斥同性戀者時
這種歧視不可忽視
始終人要獲得好
制度的公平對待只是必要條件
無形的社會壓力同樣不可忽視
Lm

replies.msg:
You still confuse marriage and love.
As I know, marriage means differently to different people. You may think marriage means developed love, but this is not applicable to other people. Some people marry because of reputation, especially when a woman become pregnant before marriage; some people marry because they are tricked by their spouse, especially rich old man tricked by young beautiful girl.
To conclude, I fail to link stage of love and marriage together. Therefore, marriage is just an agreement, and it does not signify the completeness of love, if there is one.

I disagree with your view of love. Human beings are by nature players. They fuck people, not a single person; they chase people, not a single person.
Marriage appears much later, when human organize themselves. It is to ensure the productivity by adding members to family/household, to expand a social unit to grab economic benefits and power.
There is a marriage system(agreement), but there is nature(sexual needs,emotional needs), too. What I think you are wrong is misunderstand love is marriage, and despising other forms of marriage, namely two ways of polygamy. It is even laughable to claim monogamy is better than polygamy because of a wrong perception of love.

quote.msg:
// The reason why straight people should not marry relatives is not just because
their of spring might have birth defects (a problem which can be anticipated via genetic testing, and which doesn’t apply past childbearing age), but also because of the ways in which sex changes the dynamics of family life...
...There is another important disanalogy between banning incestuous marriage and banning same-sex marriage. The incest ban means that every person is forbidden to marry some people—a relatively small group—with whom he might fall in love: his relatives. By contrast, the same-sex marriage ban means that gay people are forbidden to marry anyone with whom they might fall in love. Unlike the incest ban, it reduces their pool of available marriage partners to zero—an infinitely greater restriction.// (p.66-67)

質疑：支持同性戀logically entails 亂倫合法化

反駁：
// The reason why straight people should not marry relatives is not just because
their of spring might have birth defects (a problem which can be anticipated via genetic testing, and which doesn’t apply past childbearing age), but also because of the ways in which sex changes the dynamics of family life...
...There is another important disanalogy between banning incestuous marriage and banning same-sex marriage. The incest ban means that every person is forbidden to marry some people—a relatively small group—with whom he might fall in love: his relatives. By contrast, the same-sex marriage ban means that gay people are forbidden to marry anyone with whom they might fall in love. Unlike the incest ban, it reduces their pool of available marriage partners to zero—an infinitely greater restriction.// (p.66-67)

John Corvino, Maggie Gallagher - Debating Same-Sex Marriage (2012)
